front: Define the norm (length) of a vector in Rⁿ
back: The norm or length of a vector v = (v₁, v₂, ..., vₙ) in Rⁿ is defined as ||v|| = √(v₁² + v₂² + ... + vₙ²). This is also called the Euclidean norm.

front: What is the dot product (inner product) of two vectors?
back: The dot product of u = (u₁, u₂, ..., uₙ) and v = (v₁, v₂, ..., vₙ) is u·v = u₁v₁ + u₂v₂ + ... + uₙvₙ. It is a scalar quantity.
Two vectors u and v are orthogonal if their dot product is zero: u·v = 0. Geometrically, they are perpendicular to each other.
For any vectors u and v in Rⁿ, |u·v| ≤ ||u|| ||v||. Equality holds if and only if u and v are linearly dependent.
For any vectors u and v in Rⁿ, ||u + v|| ≤ ||u|| + ||v||. The sum of lengths of two sides of a triangle is greater than or equal to the third side.

front: What is an orthonormal set of vectors?
back: A set of vectors is orthonormal if all vectors are mutually orthogonal (dot product = 0) and each vector has unit length (norm = 1).
For a subspace W of Rⁿ, the orthogonal complement W⊥ consists of all vectors in Rⁿ that are orthogonal to every vector in W. W⊥ is also a subspace.

front: What is the projection of vector u onto vector v?
back: The projection of u onto v is projᵥ(u) = (u·v / v·v) v. This gives the component of u in the direction of v.
A square matrix Q is orthogonal if QᵀQ = I (identity matrix). Its columns form an orthonormal basis, and Q⁻¹ = Qᵀ.

front: What is the Pythagorean Theorem for vectors?
back: If u and v are orthogonal vectors, then ||u + v||² = ||u||² + ||v||². The square of the hypotenuse equals sum of squares of the other two sides.
For any vectors u and v, ||u + v||² + ||u - v||² = 2(||u||² + ||v||²). The sum of squares of diagonals equals twice sum of squares of adjacent sides.
A method to convert a set of linearly independent vectors into an orthogonal (or orthonormal) set that spans the same subspace.
A unit vector is a vector with norm equal to 1. Any non-zero vector v can be converted to a unit vector by dividing by its norm: û = v/||v||.
A normal vector to a subspace W is any vector in W⊥ (orthogonal complement). It is perpendicular to all vectors in W.
The distance between vectors u and v is defined as d(u, v) = ||u - v||. This is the length of the vector connecting u to v.
